title: Competitors and suppliers
summary: 9609 A Level — competitive rivalry, supplier power, differentiation, and micro-environment analysis.
learningObjectives: Explain supplier power and input cost risk.

simpleExplanation.title: Forces close to the firm
simpleExplanation.summary: Competitors fight for market share; suppliers control key inputs — both shape margins and strategic options.
steps: Few suppliers increase input costs and risk.

flashcards.back: Many competitors, slow growth, similar products, high fixed costs.
Few suppliers, unique input, switching costly, forward integration threat.
Less price competition; brand loyalty; premium pricing possible.
Firm owns supplier or distributor — secures supply, captures margin.
Competitors/suppliers are key forces in industry analysis (extends PESTLE).
Cash cows fund fight in star/question mark markets.

sections.content: **Competitors and suppliers** (6.1.5) is the **micro-environment** within PESTLE. Use **case names and data** — market share %, supplier concentration.

sections.question: Smartphone market: 5 global brands hold 80% share; screens supplied by 2 manufacturers. Analyse implications for a mid-size phone maker.
sections.solution:
**Competition:** **Intense rivalry** — price wars, rapid innovation cycles, high marketing spend; mid-size firm needs **niche differentiation** (3.1.5) or **feature focus**.

**Suppliers:** **Dual-source risk** — screen shortages or **price hikes** hit all rivals; mid-size firm has **weak bargaining power** vs giants.

**Strategies:** Long-term **supplier contracts**; invest in **unique software/brand**; avoid head-on **price competition** with leaders.
